rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Continued Hearing Re Defendants Fourth Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Supplemental Responses To Form Interrogatories And Demand For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday April 1, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SAN MATEO, CELIA MORENO, M.D.'S Fourth Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Supplemental Responses To Form Interrogatories And Demand For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. The code requires that plaintiff make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information to answer discovery. Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th1496 "A party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under [her] control." Plaintiff must also make a "diligent search" and a "reasonable inquiry" in responding to a document request. C.C.P. 2031.230 Plaintiff has had two years to properly respond to the discovery. Yet, despite the numerous opportunities to respond, she has violated three separate orders. Therefore, defendant's motion for terminating sanctions is granted. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is granted. Plaintiff to pay defendant County of San Mateo, Celia Moreno, M.D. and their attorneys of record Howard, Rome, Martin & Ridley $1,600.00 plus $60 in costs for a total of $1660.00 no later than May 1, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS HARRIS SR. et al VS. CELESTE SPILLANE MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Excluding Plaintiffs Expert Testimony At Trial; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration And Exhibits
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday April 1, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN, INC., THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'S Motion For Order Excluding Plaintiffs Expert Testimony At Trial. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion unopposed. Plaintiff failed to timely disclose experts and has not brought a timely motion for belated submission of experts pursuant to C.C.P. §2034.710. Therefore, the motion for order excluding plaintiffs' expert testimony at trial is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEKI LANIER, LLC, AN IDAHO LIMITED LIABILITY VS. THOMAS BROWN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Relief From Clerks Entry Of Default And For Leave To Defend The Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 1, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT THOMAS BROWN Motion For Relief From Clerks Entry Of Default And For Leave To Defend The Action. The court grants the motion to vacate per CCP 473.5. First, this motion does not violate CCP 1008 because the ex parte application was denied without prejudice. The court generally does not resolve a contested motion to vacate on the merits on the ex parte calendar. Second, defendant's declaration clearly states that he lacked notice of this action and there is a liberal policy to resolve cases on the merits. Third, the motion is timely and defendant provides a proposed answer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday April 1, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT EKO VENTURE, INC.'S Motion To Compel Psychiatric Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. rulings.Case Title: YONG ZHI PAN VS. SW HOTEL, INC. DBA SAM WONG HOTEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Interrogatory And Document Responses; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorneys
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 1, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT SW HOTEL, INC. DBA SAM WONG HOTEL's Motion To Compel Further Interrogatory And Document Responses; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorneys. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to interrogatories and requests for production of documents and their request for sanctions is as follows: Form Interrogatories and Special Interrogatories: Granted. The court finds that plaintiff did not make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information to answer discovery. See Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th1496 "A party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under [her] control." That includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Sup. Ct. (1961) 189 CA 2d 6, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 161 CA 3d 15,167-168, family members Jones v. Superior Court (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552 and experts who have been retained by a party and designated as a trial witness. Sigerseth v. Superior Court (1972) 23 CA 3d 427,433. See Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2009) 8:1051-1060 Plaintiff to serve verified responses, including all subparts, to Form Interrogatories Form #7.1, 7.2, 7.3 and Special Interrogatories #2, 10, 10, 20, 24, 25, 26, 27, and 28 no later than May 15, 2015. (Continued to part 2 of 2.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: YONG ZHI PAN VS. SW HOTEL, INC. DBA SAM WONG HOTEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sw Hotel, Inc. Dba Sam Wong Hotel Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Interrogatory And Document Responses; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorneys (Duplicate Entry - For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 1, 2015, Line 6, Defendant SW Hotel, Inc. Dba Sam Wong Hotel's Motion To Compel Further Interrogatory And Document Responses; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorneys. (Duplicate Entry - For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only.) (Part 2 of 2.) Request for Production of Documents: Granted. The court finds that the document requests are tied to documents identified in the form and special interrogatories. Since plaintiff is being ordered to serve verified responses to the form and special interrogatories, then she must update her response and production to the document request. The court also find that plaintiff failed to do a diligent search and make a reasonable inquiry in responding to the document request as is required by C.C.P. § 2031.220-240. Plaintiff to serve verified responses produce documents to Request for Production of Documents 1, 2, 11, 12, 17 and 18 no later than May 15, 2015. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted. Plaintiff and his attorney of record SHL Law Group is to $965.00 in sanctions to SW Hotel, Inc. DBA Sam Wong Hotel and its attorneys of record, Vogl, Meredith Burke in the amount of $965.00 no later than May 15, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)
